description: Comprehensive dialogue metadata for all speakers

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let there be light

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide between waters and waters

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let the waters under the heavens be gathered to one place, and let the dry land appear

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let the earth sprout vegetation, plants bearing seed, fruit trees bearing fruit after their kind with seed in it, on the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let there be lights in the firmament of the heavens to divide between the day and between the night, and let them be for signs and for seasons and for days and years

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: and let them be for lights in the firmament of the heavens to give light upon the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let the waters swarm with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the face of the firmament of the heavens

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let the earth bring forth living creatures after their kind: livestock and creeping things and beasts of the earth after their kind

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Let us make man in our image, after our likeness, and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the heavens and over the livestock and over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creeps on the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth and subdue it, and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the heavens and over every living thing that moves on the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the face of all the earth, and every tree with seed in its fruit; you shall have them for food

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the heavens and to everything that creeps on the earth, everything that has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: From every tree of the garden you may surely eat

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: but from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him

dialogue.speaker: adam
dialogue.englishText: This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man

dialogue.speaker: serpent
dialogue.englishText: Did God actually say, 'You shall not eat of any tree in the garden'?

dialogue.speaker: eve
dialogue.englishText: We may eat of the fruit of the trees in the garden

dialogue.speaker: eve
dialogue.englishText: but God said, 'You shall not eat of the fruit of the tree that is in the midst of the garden, neither shall you touch it, lest you die'

dialogue.speaker: serpent
dialogue.englishText: You will not surely die

dialogue.speaker: serpent
dialogue.englishText: For God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: Where are you?

dialogue.speaker: adam
dialogue.englishText: I heard the sound of you in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked, and I hid myself

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten of the tree of which I commanded you not to eat?

dialogue.speaker: adam
dialogue.englishText: The woman whom you gave to be with me, she gave me fruit of the tree, and I ate

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: What is this that you have done?

dialogue.speaker: eve
dialogue.englishText: The serpent deceived me, and I ate

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: Because you have done this, cursed are you above all livestock and above all beasts of the field; on your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and her offspring; he shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: I will surely multiply your pain in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children. Your desire shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: Because you have listened to the voice of your wife and have eaten of the tree of which I commanded you, saying, 'You shall not eat of it', cursed is the ground because of you; in pain you shall eat of it all the days of your life

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: Thorns and thistles it shall bring forth for you, and you shall eat the plants of the field

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread, till you return to the ground, for out of it you were taken; for you are dust, and to dust you shall return

dialogue.speaker: yhwh-god
dialogue.englishText: Behold, the man has become like one of us in knowing good and evil. Now, lest he reach out his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat, and live forever

dialogue.speaker: eve
dialogue.englishText: I have gotten a man with the help of YHWH

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Why are you angry, and why has your face fallen?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is crouching at the door. Its desire is for you, but you must rule over it

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Where is Abel your brother?

dialogue.speaker: cain
dialogue.englishText: I do not know; am I my brother's keeper?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: What have you done? The voice of your brother's blood is crying to me from the ground

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: When you work the ground, it shall no longer yield to you its strength. You shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth

dialogue.speaker: cain
dialogue.englishText: My punishment is greater than I can bear

dialogue.speaker: cain
dialogue.englishText: Behold, you have driven me today away from the ground, and from your face I shall be hidden. I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Therefore, if anyone kills Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold

dialogue.speaker: lamech-cainite
dialogue.englishText: Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; you wives of Lamech, listen to what I say: I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for striking me

dialogue.speaker: lamech-cainite
dialogue.englishText: If Cain's revenge is sevenfold, then Lamech's is seventy-sevenfold

dialogue.speaker: eve
dialogue.englishText: God has appointed for me another offspring instead of Abel, for Cain killed him

dialogue.speaker: lamech-sethite
dialogue.englishText: Out of the ground that YHWH has cursed, this one shall bring us relief from our work and from the painful toil of our hands

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: My Spirit shall not strive with man forever, since he also is flesh; his days shall be one hundred and twenty years

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will blot out man whom I have created from the face of the land, from man to animals to creeping things and to birds of the sky; for I am sorry that I have made them

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: The end of all flesh has come before Me; for the earth is filled with violence because of them; and behold, I am about to destroy them with the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Make for yourself an ark of gopher wood; you shall make the ark with rooms, and shall cover it inside and out with pitch

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: This is how you shall make it: the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its breadth fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: You shall make a window for the ark, and finish it to a cubit from the top; and set the door of the ark in the side of it; you shall make it with lower, second, and third decks

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I, even I am bringing the flood of water upon the earth, to destroy all flesh in which is the breath of life, from under heaven; everything that is on the earth shall perish

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: But I will establish My covenant with you; and you shall enter the ark — you and your sons and your wife, and your sons' wives with you

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And of every living thing of all flesh, you shall bring two of every kind into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Of the birds after their kind, and of the animals after their kind, of every creeping thing of the ground after its kind, two of every kind will come to you to keep them alive

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: As for you, take for yourself some of all food which is edible, and gather it to yourself; and it shall be for food for you and for them

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Enter, you and all your household, into the ark; for you alone I have seen to be righteous before Me in this time

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: You shall take with you of every clean animal by sevens, a male and his female; and of the animals that are not clean two, a male and his female

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Also of the birds of the sky, sevens and sevens, male and female, to keep offspring alive on the face of all the earth

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: For after seven more days, I will send rain on the earth forty days and forty nights; and I will blot out from the face of the land every living thing that I have made

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Go out of the ark, you and your wife and your sons and your sons' wives with you

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Bring out with you every living thing of all flesh that is with you, birds and animals and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth, that they may breed abundantly on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply on the earth

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will never again curse the ground on account of man, for the intent of man's heart is evil from his youth; and I will never again destroy every living thing, as I have done

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: While the earth remains, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: The fear of you and the terror of you will be on every beast of the earth and on every bird of the sky; with everything that creeps on the ground, and all the fish of the sea, into your hand they are given

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Every moving thing that is alive shall be food for you; I give all to you, as I gave the green plant

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Only you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Surely I will require your lifeblood; from every beast I will require it, and from man, from every man's brother I will require the life of man

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Whoever sheds man's blood, by man his blood shall be shed, for in the image of God He made man

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: As for you, be fruitful and multiply; populate the earth abundantly and multiply in it

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Now behold, I Myself do establish My covenant with you, and with your descendants after you

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And with every living creature that is with you, the birds, the cattle, and every beast of the earth with you; of all that comes out of the ark, even every beast of the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I establish My covenant with you; and all flesh shall never again be cut off by the water of the flood, neither shall there again be a flood to destroy the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: This is the sign of the covenant which I am making between Me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all successive generations

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I set My bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a sign of a covenant between Me and the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: It shall come about, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow will be seen in the cloud

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And I will remember My covenant, which is between Me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and never again shall the water become a flood to destroy all flesh

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: When the bow is in the cloud, then I will look upon it, to remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: This is the sign of the covenant which I have established between Me and all flesh that is on the earth

dialogue.speaker: noah
dialogue.englishText: Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants he shall be to his brothers

dialogue.speaker: noah
dialogue.englishText: Blessed be YHWH, the God of Shem; and let Canaan be his servant

dialogue.speaker: noah
dialogue.englishText: May God enlarge Japheth, and let him dwell in the tents of Shem; and let Canaan be his servant

dialogue.speaker: people-of-babel
dialogue.englishText: Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly

dialogue.speaker: people-of-babel
dialogue.englishText: Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered over the face of the whole earth

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Behold, they are one people and they all have one language, and this is only the beginning of what they will do; nothing they propose to do will now be impossible for them

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Come, let us go down and there confuse their language so that they may not understand one another's speech

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Go from your country and your kindred and your father's house to the land that I will show you

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: And I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will bless those who bless you, and him who dishonors you I will curse, and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: To your offspring I will give this land

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: See, I know that you are a woman beautiful in appearance

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: When the Egyptians see you they will say, this is his wife, and they will kill me but they will let you live

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Say you are my sister, that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared for your sake

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-abraham
dialogue.englishText: What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-abraham
dialogue.englishText: Why did you say, she is my sister, so that I took her for my wife? Now then, here is your wife; take her, and go

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herdsmen and my herdsmen, for we are kinsmen

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Is not the whole land before you? Separate yourself from me. If you take the left hand, then I will go to the right, or if you take the right hand, then I will go to the left

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Lift up your eyes and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: For all the land that you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will make your offspring as the dust of the earth, so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your offspring also can be counted

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Arise, walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I will give it to you

dialogue.speaker: melchizedek
dialogue.englishText: Blessed be Abram by God Most High, possessor of heaven and earth

dialogue.speaker: melchizedek
dialogue.englishText: And blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand

dialogue.speaker: king-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Give me the persons, but take the goods for yourself

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: I have lifted my hand to YHWH, God Most High, possessor of heaven and earth

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: that I would not take a thread or a sandal strap or anything that is yours, lest you should say, I have made Abram rich

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: I will take nothing but what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me — Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre — let them take their share

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Fear not, Abram, I am your shield; your reward shall be very great

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: O Lord YHWH, what will you give me, for I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Behold, you have given me no offspring, and a member of my household will be my heir

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: This man shall not be your heir; your very own son shall be your heir

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Look toward heaven, and number the stars, if you are able to number them

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: So shall your offspring be

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I am YHWH who brought you out from Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to possess

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: O Lord YHWH, how am I to know that I shall possess it?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Bring me a heifer three years old, a female goat three years old, a ram three years old, a turtledove, and a young pigeon

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Know for certain that your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: But I will bring judgment on the nation that they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: As for you, you shall go to your fathers in peace; you shall be buried in a good old age

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: And they shall come back here in the fourth generation, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: To your offspring I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: Behold now, YHWH has restrained me from bearing; please go in to my maidservant; perhaps I will be built up through her

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: My wrong be upon you! I gave my maidservant into your embrace; and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes. May YHWH judge between me and you

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Behold, your maidservant is in your hand; do to her what is good in your eyes

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Where have you come from and where are you going?

dialogue.speaker: hagar
dialogue.englishText: From the presence of Sarai my mistress I am fleeing

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Return to your mistress and submit yourself under her hands

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will greatly multiply your offspring so that they cannot be counted for multitude

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Behold, you are pregnant and will bear a son; you shall call his name Ishmael, because YHWH has heard your affliction

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: He will be a wild donkey of a man; his hand against everyone and everyone's hand against him, and he will dwell before all his brothers

dialogue.speaker: hagar
dialogue.englishText: You are a God of seeing, for she said: Have I truly seen here after he who sees me?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I am God Almighty; walk before me and be blameless

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: And I will make my covenant between me and you, and will multiply you exceedingly

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: As for me, behold my covenant is with you, and you shall be the father of a multitude of nations

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: No longer shall your name be called Abram, but your name shall be Abraham, for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come from you

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And I will establish my covenant between me and you and your offspring after you throughout their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your offspring after you

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And I will give to you and to your offspring after you the land of your sojournings, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession, and I will be their God

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: This is my covenant which you shall keep, between me and you and your offspring after you: every male among you shall be circumcised

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: You shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Every male throughout your generations who is eight days old shall be circumcised among you, whether born in the house or bought with money from any foreigner who is not of your offspring

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: He who is born in your house and he who is bought with your money shall surely be circumcised; so shall my covenant be in your flesh an everlasting covenant

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Any uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, for Sarah is her name

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I will bless her, and moreover I will give you a son by her; I will bless her and she shall become nations; kings of peoples shall come from her

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Oh that Ishmael might live before you!

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: No, Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac; I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his offspring after him

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: As for Ishmael, I have heard you; behold, I have blessed him and will make him fruitful and multiply him greatly; he shall father twelve princes and I will make him into a great nation

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this set time next year

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: My lord, if I have found favor in your sight, do not pass by your servant

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: And let me bring a morsel of bread that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on — since you have come to your servant

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: So do, as you have said

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Quick! Three seahs of fine flour! Knead it and make cakes

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Where is Sarah your wife?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: There, in the tent

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will surely return to you at this time next year, and behold, Sarah your wife shall have a son

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: After I am worn out, shall I have pleasure, when my lord is old also?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Why did Sarah laugh, saying: Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Is anything too hard for YHWH? At the appointed time I will return to you, about this time next year, and Sarah shall have a son

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: I did not laugh, for I was afraid

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: No, but you did laugh

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Shall I hide from Abraham what I am doing?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: For I have chosen him, that he may command his children and his household after him to keep the way of YHWH by doing righteousness and justice, so that YHWH may bring to Abraham what he has promised him

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: The outcry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and their sin is very grave

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will go down to see whether they have done altogether as the outcry that has come to me — and if not, I will know

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city; will you also sweep away and not spare the place for the fifty righteous who are in it?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Far be it from you to do such a thing, to put the righteous to death with the wicked, so that the righteous fare as the wicked! Far be that from you! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: If I find at Sodom fifty righteous in the city, I will spare all the place for their sake

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have undertaken to speak to the Lord, I who am but dust and ashes

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Suppose five of the fifty righteous are lacking; will you destroy the whole city for lack of five?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Suppose forty are found there

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: For the sake of forty I will not do it

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak: Suppose thirty are found there

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will not do it if I find thirty there

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have undertaken to speak to the Lord: Suppose twenty are found there

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: For the sake of twenty I will not destroy it

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak again but this once: Suppose ten are found there

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: For the sake of ten I will not destroy it

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: My lords, please turn aside to your servant's house and spend the night and wash your feet; then you may rise early and go on your way

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: No, we will spend the night in the town square

dialogue.speaker: men-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, that we may know them

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have two daughters who have not known any man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please; only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof

dialogue.speaker: men-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Stand back!

dialogue.speaker: men-of-sodom
dialogue.englishText: This fellow came to sojourn, and he would play the judge! Now we will deal worse with you than with them

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Have you anyone else here? Son-in-law, sons, daughters, or anyone you have in the city — bring them out of the place

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: For we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before YHWH, and YHWH has sent us to destroy it

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Up, get out of this place, for YHWH is about to destroy the city

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Up! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Escape for your life; do not look back or stop anywhere in the valley; escape to the hills, lest you be swept away

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Oh no, my lords

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life; but I cannot escape to the hills, lest the disaster overtake me and I die

dialogue.speaker: lot
dialogue.englishText: Behold, this city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one; let me escape there — is it not a little one? — and my life will be saved

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I grant you this favor also, that I will not overthrow the city of which you have spoken

dialogue.speaker: angels-at-sodom
dialogue.englishText: Escape there quickly, for I can do nothing until you arrive there

dialogue.speaker: lots-daughters
dialogue.englishText: Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us after the manner of all the earth

dialogue.speaker: lots-daughters
dialogue.englishText: Come, let us make our father drink wine and we will lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father

dialogue.speaker: lots-daughters
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine tonight also, and you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve offspring from our father

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Behold, you are a dead man because of the woman whom you have taken, for she is a man's wife

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Lord, will you kill an innocent people?

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Did he not himself say to me: She is my sister? And she herself said: He is my brother. In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Yes, I know that you have done this in the integrity of your heart, and it was I who kept you from sinning against me; therefore I did not let you touch her

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Now then, return the man's wife, for he is a prophet, so that he will pray for you and you shall live. But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die, you and all who are yours

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: What have you done to us? And how have I sinned against you, that you have brought on me and my kingdom a great sin? You have done to me things that ought not to be done

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: What did you see, that you did this thing?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: I did it because I thought there is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Besides, she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father, though not the daughter of my mother, and she became my wife

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: When God caused me to wander from my father's house, I said to her: This is the kindness you must do me — at every place to which we come, say of me: He is my brother

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Behold, my land is before you; dwell where it pleases you

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; it is a sign of your innocence in the eyes of all who are with you, and before everyone you are vindicated

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: God has made laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh with me

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? For I have borne him a son in his old age

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.englishText: Cast out this slave woman and her son, for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son, with Isaac

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Do not be distressed because of the boy and because of your slave woman; whatever Sarah says to you, listen to her voice, for through Isaac shall your offspring be called

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: And also the son of the slave woman I will make into a nation, because he is your offspring

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-god
dialogue.englishText: What troubles you, Hagar? Fear not, for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-god
dialogue.englishText: Rise up, lift up the boy and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make him into a great nation

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: God is with you in all that you do

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my offspring or with my posterity; but as I have dealt kindly with you, so you will deal with me and with the land where you have sojourned

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: I swear

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: I do not know who has done this thing; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: What are these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: These seven ewe lambs you shall take from my hand, so that it may be a witness for me that I dug this well

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Abraham!

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Here I am

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains of which I shall tell you

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Stay here with the donkey; I and the boy will go over there and worship and come back to you

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: My father!

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Here I am, my son

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: God will provide for himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Abraham! Abraham!

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Here I am

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him, for now I know that you fear God, seeing you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: By myself I have sworn, declares YHWH, because you have done this thing and have not withheld your son, your only son

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will surely bless you and I will surely multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven and as the sand on the seashore. And your offspring shall possess the gate of his enemies

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: And in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because you have obeyed my voice

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: I am a sojourner and foreigner among you; give me property among you for a burying place, that I may bury my dead out of my sight

dialogue.speaker: sons-of-heth
dialogue.englishText: Hear us, my lord; you are a prince of God among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will withhold his tomb from you to hinder you from burying your dead

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: If you are willing that I should bury my dead out of my sight, hear me and entreat for me Ephron the son of Zohar

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: That he may give me the cave of Machpelah, which he owns; it is at the end of his field. For the full price let him give it to me in your presence as property for a burying place

dialogue.speaker: ephron
dialogue.englishText: No, my lord, hear me: I give you the field, and I give you the cave that is in it. In the sight of the sons of my people I give it to you. Bury your dead

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: But if you will, hear me: I give the price of the field; accept it from me, that I may bury my dead there

dialogue.speaker: ephron
dialogue.englishText: My lord, listen to me: a piece of land worth four hundred shekels of silver, what is that between you and me? Bury your dead

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: Put your hand under my thigh

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: That I may make you swear by YHWH, God of heaven and earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites among whom I dwell

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: But will go to my country and to my kindred, and take a wife for my son Isaac

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Perhaps the woman will not be willing to follow me to this land. Must I then take your son back to the land from which you came?

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: See to it that you do not take my son back there

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: YHWH, God of heaven, who took me from my father's house and from the land of my kindred, and who spoke to me and swore to me: To your offspring I will give this land — he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there

dialogue.speaker: abraham
dialogue.englishText: But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you will be free from this oath of mine; only you must not take my son back there

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: O YHWH, God of my master Abraham, please grant me success today and show steadfast love to my master Abraham

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Let the young woman to whom I shall say: Please let down your jar that I may drink — and who shall say: Drink, and I will water your camels also — let her be the one you have appointed for your servant Isaac. By this I shall know that you have shown steadfast love to my master

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Please give me a little water to drink from your jar

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Drink, my lord

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: I will draw water for your camels also, until they have finished drinking

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Whose daughter are you? Tell me: is there room in your father's house for us to spend the night?

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: We have plenty of both straw and fodder, and room to spend the night

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Blessed be YHWH, the God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken his steadfast love and his faithfulness toward my master. As for me, YHWH has led me in the way to the house of my master's kinsmen

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Come in, O blessed of YHWH. Why do you stand outside? For I have prepared the house and a place for the camels

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: I will not eat until I have said what I have to say

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Speak

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: I am Abraham's servant

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: YHWH has greatly blessed my master, and he has become great. He has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male servants and female servants, camels and donkeys

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: And Sarah my master's wife bore a son to my master when she was old, and to him he has given all that he has

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: My master made me swear, saying: You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I dwell

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: But you shall go to my father's house and to my clan and take a wife for my son

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: I said to my master: Perhaps the woman will not follow me

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: He said to me: YHWH, before whom I have walked, will send his angel with you and prosper your way. You shall take a wife for my son from my clan and from my father's house

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Then you will be free from my oath, when you come to my clan. And if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: I came today to the spring and said: O YHWH, God of my master Abraham, if now you are prospering the way that I go

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water. Let the virgin who comes out to draw water, to whom I shall say: Please give me a little water from your jar to drink

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: And who will say to me: Drink you, and I will draw for your camels also — let her be the woman whom YHWH has appointed for my master's son

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Before I had finished speaking in my heart, behold, Rebekah came out with her jar on her shoulder, and she went down to the spring and drew. I said to her: Please let me drink

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: She quickly lowered her jar from her shoulder and said: Drink, and I will water your camels also. So I drank and she watered the camels

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Then I asked her: Whose daughter are you? She said: The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore to him. So I put the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her arms

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Then I bowed my head and worshiped YHWH and blessed YHWH, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me by the right way to take the daughter of my master's kinsman for his son

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Now then, if you are going to show steadfast love and faithfulness to my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, that I may turn to the right hand or to the left

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: The thing has come from YHWH; we cannot speak to you bad or good

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Behold, Rebekah is before you; take her and go, and let her be the wife for your master's son, as YHWH has spoken

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Send me away to my master

dialogue.speaker: rebekahs-family
dialogue.englishText: Let the young woman remain with us a while, at least ten days; after that she may go

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: Do not delay me, since YHWH has prospered my way. Send me away that I may go to my master

dialogue.speaker: rebekahs-family
dialogue.englishText: Let us call the young woman and ask her

dialogue.speaker: rebekahs-family
dialogue.englishText: Will you go with this man?

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: I will go

dialogue.speaker: rebekahs-family
dialogue.englishText: Our sister, may you become thousands of ten thousands, and may your offspring possess the gate of those who hate him

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Who is that man, walking in the field to meet us?

dialogue.speaker: abrahams-servant
dialogue.englishText: It is my master

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: If it is thus, why is this happening to me?

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples from within you shall be divided; the one shall be stronger than the other, the older shall serve the younger

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Let me eat some of that red stew, for I am exhausted

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Sell me your birthright now

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: I am about to die; of what use is a birthright to me?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Swear to me now

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Do not go down to Egypt; dwell in the land which I will tell you of

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and will bless you; for to you and to your offspring I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath which I swore to Abraham your father

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I will multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven and will give to your offspring all these lands; and by your offspring all the nations of the earth shall bless themselves

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Because Abraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: She is my sister

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Surely she is your wife! How then did you say, She is my sister?

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Because I thought, lest I die because of her

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Whoever touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we

dialogue.speaker: gerar-shepherds
dialogue.englishText: The water is ours

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: For now YHWH has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I am the God of Abraham your father; fear not, for I am with you and will bless you and multiply your offspring for my servant Abraham's sake

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: We see plainly that YHWH has been with you, so we said: let there be an oath between us and you, and let us make a covenant with you

dialogue.speaker: abimelech
dialogue.englishText: That you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you and have done to you nothing but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of YHWH

dialogue.speaker: isaac-servants
dialogue.englishText: We have found water

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: My son!

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Here I am

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I am old; I do not know the day of my death

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Now then, take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: And make me savory food, such as I love, and bring it to me that I may eat, so that my soul may bless you before I die

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I heard your father speaking to your brother Esau, saying:

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Bring me game and make me savory food, that I may eat and bless you before YHWH before my death

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice as I command you

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Go to the flock and bring me two good young goats, so that I may prepare from them savory food for your father, such as he loves

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: And you shall bring it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before his death

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be mocking him and bring a curse upon myself and not a blessing

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Let your curse be on me, my son; only obey my voice, and go

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My father!

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Here I am. Who are you, my son?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am Esau your firstborn. I have done as you told me; now sit up and eat of my game, that your soul may bless me

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Because YHWH your God granted me success

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, whether you are really my son Esau or not

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Are you really my son Esau?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Bring it near to me that I may eat of my son's game, so that my soul may bless you

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Come near and kiss me, my son

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field that YHWH has blessed

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: May God give you of the dew of heaven and of the fatness of the earth and plenty of grain and wine

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and may your mother's sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be everyone who blesses you

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Let my father arise and eat of his son's game, that your soul may bless me

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Who are you?

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: I am your son, your firstborn, Esau

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him? Indeed he shall be blessed

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Bless me, even me also, O my father!

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Your brother came deceitfully and took away your blessing

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright, and behold, now he has taken away my blessing

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have made him lord over you, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants, and with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Have you but one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, O my father!

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall your dwelling be, and away from the dew of heaven on high

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; but when you grow restless you shall break his yoke from your neck

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Behold, your brother Esau comforts himself about you by planning to kill you

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice. Arise, flee to Laban my brother in Haran

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: And stay with him a few days, until your brother's fury turns away

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: Until your brother's anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send and fetch you from there. Why should I be bereft of you both in one day?

dialogue.speaker: rebekah
dialogue.englishText: I am weary of my life because of the Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women such as these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: Arise, go to Paddan-aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother's father, and take as your wife from there one of the daughters of Laban your mother's brother

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, that you may become a company of peoples

dialogue.speaker: isaac
dialogue.englishText: May he give you the blessing of Abraham, to you and to your offspring with you, that you may take possession of the land of your sojournings that God gave to Abraham

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: I am YHWH, the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac. The land on which you lie I will give to you and to your offspring

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Your offspring shall be like the dust of the earth, and you shall spread abroad to the west and to the east and to the north and to the south, and in you and your offspring shall all the families of the earth be blessed

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land. For I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Surely YHWH is in this place, and I did not know it

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If God will be with me and will keep me in this way that I go, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: So that I come again to my father's house in peace, then YHWH shall be my God

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: And this stone, which I have set up for a pillar, shall be God's house. And of all that you give me I will give a full tenth to you

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My brothers, where are you from?

dialogue.speaker: shepherds
dialogue.englishText: We are from Haran

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Do you know Laban the son of Nahor?

dialogue.speaker: shepherds
dialogue.englishText: We know him

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Is it well with him?

dialogue.speaker: shepherds
dialogue.englishText: It is well; and behold, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Behold, it is still high day; it is not time for the livestock to be gathered. Water the sheep and go, pasture them

dialogue.speaker: shepherds
dialogue.englishText: We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together and the stone is rolled from the mouth of the well; then we water the sheep

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Surely you are my bone and my flesh!

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Because you are my kinsman, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I will serve you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: It is better that I give her to you than to any other man; stay with me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Give me my wife that I may go in to her, for my time is completed

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: It is not so done in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Because YHWH has looked upon my affliction; for now my husband will love me

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Because YHWH has heard that I am hated, he has given me this son also

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Now this time my husband will be attached to me, because I have borne him three sons

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: This time I will praise YHWH

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: Give me children, or else I die!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: Here is my maidservant Bilhah; go in to her, so that she may bear on my knees and even I may have children through her

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: God has judged me, and has also heard my voice and given me a son

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister and have prevailed

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Good fortune has come!

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Happy am I! For women will call me happy

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: Please give me some of your son's mandrakes

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Is it a small matter that you have taken away my husband? Would you take away my son's mandrakes also?

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: Then he may lie with you tonight in exchange for your son's mandrakes

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: Come in to me, for I have hired you with my son's mandrakes

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: God has given me my wages because I gave my maidservant to my husband

dialogue.speaker: leah
dialogue.englishText: God has endowed me with a good endowment; now my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: God has taken away my reproach

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: May YHWH add to me another son!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Send me away, that I may go to my own place and to my own country

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, and let me go. For you know my service that I have done for you

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: If I have found favor in your sight, I have learned by divination that YHWH has blessed me because of you

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Name your wages, and I will give it

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: You yourself know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: For you had little before I came, and it has increased abundantly, and YHWH has blessed you wherever I turned. But now when shall I provide for my own household also?

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: What shall I give you?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: You shall not give me anything. If you will do this thing for me, I will again pasture your flock and keep it

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Let me pass through all your flock today, removing from it every speckled and spotted sheep and every black lamb, and the spotted and speckled among the goats, and they shall be my wages

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: So my honesty will answer for me later, when you come to look into my wages with you. Every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats and black among the lambs, if found with me, shall be counted stolen

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Good! Let it be as you have said

dialogue.speaker: yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Return to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I see that your father's face is not toward me as before, but the God of my father has been with me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: You know that I have served your father with all my strength

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Yet your father has cheated me and changed my wages ten times; but God did not allow him to harm me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If he said, 'The speckled shall be your wages,' then all the flock bore speckled; and if he said, 'The striped shall be your wages,' then all the flock bore striped

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Thus God has taken away the livestock of your father and given them to me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: In the breeding season I lifted up my eyes and saw in a dream that the he-goats that mated with the flock were striped, speckled, and spotted

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-god
dialogue.englishText: Jacob!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Here I am

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-god
dialogue.englishText: Lift up your eyes and see — all the he-goats mating with the flock are striped, speckled, and spotted, for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-god
dialogue.englishText: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and made a vow to me; now arise, go out from this land and return to the land of your birth

dialogue.speaker: rachel-and-leah
dialogue.englishText: Is there any portion or inheritance left to us in our father's house?

dialogue.speaker: rachel-and-leah
dialogue.englishText: Are we not regarded by him as foreigners? For he has sold us and has indeed consumed our money

dialogue.speaker: rachel-and-leah
dialogue.englishText: All the wealth that God has taken away from our father belongs to us and to our children; now then, whatever God has said to you, do

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Be careful not to speak to Jacob either good or bad

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: What have you done, that you have tricked me and driven away my daughters like captives of the sword?

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Why did you flee secretly and trick me, and did not tell me so I could have sent you away with joy and songs, with tambourine and lyre?

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: And you did not permit me to kiss my sons and my daughters farewell. Now you have done foolishly

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: It is in my power to do you harm; but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying: Be careful not to speak to Jacob either good or bad

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: And now you have gone away because you longed greatly for your father's house — but why did you steal my gods?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Because I was afraid, for I thought you would take your daughters from me by force

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Anyone with whom you find your gods shall not live; in the presence of our kinsmen, point out what I have with me and take it

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.englishText: Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: What is my transgression? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: For you have felt through all my goods; what have you found of all your household goods? Set it here before my kinsmen and your kinsmen, that they may decide between us two

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams of your flocks

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: What was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it myself. From my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: There I was: by day the heat consumed me, and the cold by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: These twenty years I have been in your house: I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty. God saw my affliction and the labor of my hands and rebuked you last night

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day for these my daughters or for their children whom they have borne?

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it be a witness between you and me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Gather stones

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: This heap is a witness between you and me today. Therefore he named it Gilead

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: And the Mizpah, for he said: May the LORD watch between you and me, when we are out of one another's sight

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: If you afflict my daughters, or if you take wives besides my daughters, although no man is with us, see, God is witness between you and me

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: See this heap and see the pillar, which I have set between you and me

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: This heap is a witness, and the pillar is a witness, that I will not pass over this heap to you, and you will not pass over this heap and this pillar to me, for harm

dialogue.speaker: laban
dialogue.englishText: The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: This is God's camp!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Thus you shall say to my lord Esau: Thus says your servant Jacob, I have sojourned with Laban and stayed until now

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I have oxen, donkeys, flocks, male servants, and female servants; I have sent to tell my lord, in order to find favor in your sight

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-messengers
dialogue.englishText: We came to your brother Esau, and he is coming to meet you, and there are four hundred men with him

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If Esau comes to the one camp and attacks it, then the camp that is left will escape

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, O LORD who said to me, Return to your country and to your kindred, that I may do you good

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am not worthy of the least of all the deeds of steadfast love and all the faithfulness that you have shown to your servant, for with only my staff I crossed this Jordan, and now I have become two camps

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I fear him, that he may come and attack me, mothers with children

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: But you said, I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Pass on ahead of me and put space between drove and drove

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: When Esau my brother meets you and asks you, To whom do you belong? Where are you going? And whose are these ahead of you?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Then you shall say, They belong to your servant Jacob. It is a present sent to my lord Esau; and moreover he is behind us

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Speak in this manner to Esau when you find him

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: And you shall say, Moreover your servant Jacob is behind us. For he thought, I will appease him with the present that goes ahead of me, and afterward I will see his face; perhaps he will accept me

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Let me go, for the dawn has broken

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I will not let you go unless you bless me

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: What is your name?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Jacob

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with men, and have prevailed

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Please tell me your name

dialogue.speaker: angel-of-yhwh
dialogue.englishText: Why is it that you ask my name?

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Who are these with you?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The children whom God has graciously given your servant

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: What do you mean by all this company that I met?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: To find favor in the sight of my lord

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: No, please, if I have found favor in your sight, then accept my present from my hand, for truly to see your face is like seeing the face of God, and you have accepted me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Please accept my blessing that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Let us journey on our way, and I will go ahead of you

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My lord knows that the children are frail, and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me; if they are driven hard one day, all the flocks will die

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Let my lord pass on ahead of his servant, and I will lead on slowly, at the pace of the livestock that are ahead of me and at the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir

dialogue.speaker: esau
dialogue.englishText: Let me leave with you some of the people who are with me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Why is that? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord

dialogue.speaker: shechem
dialogue.englishText: Get me this girl for my wife

dialogue.speaker: hamor
dialogue.englishText: The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter. Please give her to him in marriage

dialogue.speaker: hamor
dialogue.englishText: Make marriages with us; give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves

dialogue.speaker: hamor
dialogue.englishText: You shall dwell with us, and the land shall be open to you; dwell and trade in it, and get property in it

dialogue.speaker: shechem
dialogue.englishText: Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give

dialogue.speaker: shechem
dialogue.englishText: Ask me for as great a bride price and gift as you will, and I will give whatever you say to me; only give me the girl to be my wife

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Only on this condition will we consent to you: that you will become as we are by every male among you being circumcised

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters to ourselves, and we will dwell with you and become one people

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our daughter and go

dialogue.speaker: hamor-and-shechem
dialogue.englishText: These men are at peace with us; let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for behold the land is large enough for them; let us take their daughters as wives and let us give them our daughters

dialogue.speaker: hamor-and-shechem
dialogue.englishText: Only on this condition will the men consent to dwell with us to become one people, when every male among us is circumcised as they are circumcised

dialogue.speaker: hamor-and-shechem
dialogue.englishText: Will not their livestock, their property, and all their beasts be ours? Only let us consent to them, and they will dwell with us

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: You have brought trouble on me by making me stink to the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites. My numbers are few, and if they gather themselves against me and attack me, I shall be destroyed, both I and my household

dialogue.speaker: simeon-and-levi
dialogue.englishText: Should he treat our sister like a prostitute?

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Arise, go up to Bethel and dwell there. Make an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esau

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Put away the foreign gods that are among you and purify yourselves and change your garments

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Then let us arise and go up to Bethel, so that I may make there an altar to the God who answers me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Your name is Jacob; your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I am God Almighty: be fruitful and multiply. A nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come from your own body

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: The land that I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you, and I will give the land to your offspring after you

dialogue.speaker: midwife
dialogue.englishText: Do not fear, for you have another son

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Hear now this dream that I have dreamed

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and behold, my sheaf arose and stood upright; and behold, your sheaves gathered around and bowed down to my sheaf

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Will you indeed reign over us? Will you indeed rule over us?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have dreamed another dream; and behold, the sun and the moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow down to the ground before you?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Are your brothers not pasturing the flock at Shechem? Come, I will send you to them

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Here I am

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Go now, see whether it is well with your brothers and well with the flock, and bring me word

dialogue.speaker: unnamed-man
dialogue.englishText: What are you seeking?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am seeking my brothers; tell me, please, where they are pasturing

dialogue.speaker: unnamed-man
dialogue.englishText: They have moved on from here; for I heard them say, let us go to Dothan

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Behold, this dreamer is coming

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits, and we will say a wild beast has devoured him, and we will see what will become of his dreams

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: Let us not take his life

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: Shed no blood; throw him into this pit that is in the wilderness, and lay no hand on him — in order to rescue him from their hand to return him to his father

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Come, let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let our hand not be upon him, for he is our brother, our flesh

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: The boy is gone; and I, where shall I go?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
