dialogue.englishText: This we have found; please examine whether it is your son's robe or not

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My son's robe! A wild beast has devoured him; Joseph is surely torn to pieces

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Surely I will go down to my son in Sheol mourning

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Go in to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her, and raise up offspring for your brother

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Remain a widow in your father's house until Shelah my son grows up

dialogue.speaker: unnamed-messenger
dialogue.englishText: Behold, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Come now, let me come in to you

dialogue.speaker: tamar
dialogue.englishText: What will you give me, that you may come in to me?

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: I will send a young goat from the flock

dialogue.speaker: tamar
dialogue.englishText: If you give a pledge until you send it

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: What pledge shall I give you?

dialogue.speaker: tamar
dialogue.englishText: Your signet and your cord and your staff that is in your hand

dialogue.speaker: hirah
dialogue.englishText: Where is the cult prostitute who was at Enaim on the road?

dialogue.speaker: men-of-the-place
dialogue.englishText: There has been no cult prostitute here

dialogue.speaker: hirah
dialogue.englishText: I did not find her; and also the men of the place said no cult prostitute has been here

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Let her keep the things for herself, lest we be put to shame; behold, I sent this young goat and you have not found her

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Bring her out and let her be burned

dialogue.speaker: tamar
dialogue.englishText: By the man to whom these belong, I am pregnant

dialogue.speaker: tamar
dialogue.englishText: Please identify whose these are — the signet and the cords and the staff

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to Shelah my son

dialogue.speaker: midwife
dialogue.englishText: This one came out first

dialogue.speaker: midwife
dialogue.englishText: What a breach you have made for yourself! — Perez

dialogue.speaker: potiphars-wife
dialogue.englishText: Lie with me

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, my master does not know what is in the house with me, and he has put everything that he has in my charge

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: He is not greater in this house than I, and he has withheld nothing from me except you, because you are his wife. How then could I do this great evil and sin against God?

dialogue.speaker: potiphars-wife
dialogue.englishText: Lie with me

dialogue.speaker: potiphars-wife
dialogue.englishText: See, he has brought to us a Hebrew man to laugh at us; he came in to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice

dialogue.speaker: potiphars-wife
dialogue.englishText: And when he heard that I raised my voice and cried out, he left his garment with me and fled and went outside

dialogue.speaker: potiphars-wife
dialogue.englishText: The Hebrew servant whom you brought to us came in to me to laugh at me

dialogue.speaker: potiphars-wife
dialogue.englishText: And as I raised my voice and cried out, he left his garment with me and fled outside

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Why are your faces downcast today?

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: We have dreamed a dream, and there is no one to interpret it

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Do not interpretations belong to God? Tell it to me, please

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: In my dream, behold, a vine was before me

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And on the vine were three branches; as it was budding, its blossoms shot forth, and its clusters ripened into grapes

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: In three more days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your position, and you will place Pharaoh's cup in his hand, as in the former manner when you were his cupbearer

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Only remember me when it goes well with you, and please do me the kindness to mention me to Pharaoh, and so get me out of this house

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: For I was indeed stolen out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also I have done nothing that they should put me in the pit

dialogue.speaker: baker
dialogue.englishText: I also had a dream — behold, three cake baskets were on my head

dialogue.speaker: baker
dialogue.englishText: And in the uppermost basket there were all sorts of baked food for Pharaoh, and the birds were eating them out of the basket from off my head

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: This is its interpretation: the three baskets are three days

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: In three more days Pharaoh will lift off your head from you and hang you on a tree, and the birds will eat your flesh from off you

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: My sins I remember today

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, me and the chief baker

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And we dreamed a dream in the same night, he and I, each according to the interpretation of his dream we dreamed

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And there was with us a young man, a Hebrew, a servant of the captain of the guard; and we told him, and he interpreted for us our dreams, to each man according to his dream he interpreted

dialogue.speaker: cupbearer
dialogue.englishText: And it came to pass, as he interpreted to us, so it was: me he restored to my position, and him he hanged

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: I have dreamed a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it; and I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Not I; God will give Pharaoh a favorable answer

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: In my dream, behold, I was standing on the bank of the Nile

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, out of the Nile came up seven cows, fat and attractive, and they grazed in the reed grass

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, seven other cows came up after them, poor and very ugly and thin, such as I had never seen in all the land of Egypt for ugliness

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the thin and ugly cows ate up the first seven fat cows

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: But when they had eaten them no one would have known that they had eaten them, for they were still as ugly as at the beginning; then I awoke

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: I also saw in my dream seven ears growing on one stalk, full and good

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, seven ears, withered, thin, blighted by the east wind, sprouting after them

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the thin ears swallowed up the seven good ears; and I told it to the magicians, but there was no one who could explain it to me

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Pharaoh's dream is one; what God is about to do he has revealed to Pharaoh

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years; the dream is one

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the seven lean and ugly cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears blighted by the east wind will be seven years of famine

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: This is the thing I told Pharaoh: what God is about to do he has shown to Pharaoh

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, seven years of great plenty are coming throughout all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: But after them there will arise seven years of famine, and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt; the famine will consume the land

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the plenty will be unknown in the land by reason of the famine that will follow, for it will be very severe

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And the doubling of Pharaoh's dream means that the thing is fixed by God, and God will shortly bring it about

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man, and set him over the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Let Pharaoh proceed to appoint overseers over the land and take one-fifth of the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven plentiful years

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And let them gather all the food of these good years that are coming and store up grain under the authority of Pharaoh for food in the cities, and let them keep it

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: That food shall be a reserve for the land against the seven years of famine that are to occur in the land of Egypt, so that the land may not perish through the famine

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Can we find a man like this, in whom is the Spirit of God?

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Since God has shown you all this, there is none so discerning and wise as you are

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: You shall be over my house, and all my people shall order themselves as you command; only as regards the throne will I be greater than you

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am Pharaoh, and without your consent no man shall lift up hand or foot in all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Go to Joseph. What he says to you, do

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Why do you look at one another?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have heard that there is grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy grain for us there, that we may live and not die

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Where do you come from?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: From the land of Canaan, to buy food

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: You are spies; you have come to see the nakedness of the land

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: No, my lord, your servants have come to buy food

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We are all sons of one man. We are honest men; your servants have never been spies

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: No, it is the nakedness of the land that you have come to see

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We, your servants, are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is no more

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: It is as I said to you: you are spies

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: By this you shall be tested: by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go from this place unless your youngest brother comes here

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Send one of you, and let him bring your brother, while you remain confined, that your words may be tested, whether there is truth in you. Or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Do this and you will live, for I fear God

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers remain confined where you are in custody, and let the rest go and carry grain for the famine of your households

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And bring your youngest brother to me, so your words will be verified, and you shall not die

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: In truth we are guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul when he begged us and we did not listen. That is why this distress has come upon us

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: Did I not tell you not to sin against the boy? But you did not listen. So now there comes a reckoning for his blood

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: My money has been put back; here it is in my sack!

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: What is this that God has done to us?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: The man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly to us and took us to be spies of the land

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: But we said to him, 'We are honest men; we have never been spies'

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is no more, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Then the man, the lord of the land, said to us, 'By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers here with me, and take grain for the famine of your households and go'

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: And bring your youngest brother to me, then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin. All this has come against me

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: Kill my two sons if I do not bring him back to you. Put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he is the only one left. If harm should happen to him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Go back, buy us a little food

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: The man solemnly warned us, saying, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: But if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, 'You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you'

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Why did you treat me so badly by telling the man that you had another brother?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our kindred, saying, 'Is your father still alive? Do you have another brother?' We told him in answer to these questions. Could we in any way know that he would say, 'Bring your brother down'?

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Send the boy with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die, both we and you and also our little ones

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: I will be a pledge of his safety; from my hand you shall require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame before you forever

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: If we had not delayed, we could have gone and returned twice by now

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If it must be so, then do this: take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry a present down to the man, a little balm and a little honey, gum, myrrh, pistachio nuts, and almonds

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Take double the money with you. Carry back with you the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks. Perhaps it was an oversight

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Take also your brother, and arise, go back to the man

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: May God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, and may he send back your other brother and Benjamin. And as for me, if I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Oh, my lord, we came down the first time to buy food

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: And when we came to the lodging place we opened our sacks, and there was each man's money in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; so we have brought it back with us

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: And we have brought other money down with us to buy food; we do not know who put our money in our sacks

dialogue.speaker: josephs-steward
dialogue.englishText: Peace to you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father has put treasure in your sacks for you; I received your money

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Your servant our father is well; he is still alive

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: God be gracious to you, my son

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Serve the food

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Up, follow the men, and when you overtake them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?'

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Is it not from this that my lord drinks, and by this that he practices divination? You have done evil in doing this

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing!

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Behold, the money that we found in the mouth of our sacks we brought back to you from the land of Canaan. How then could we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Whichever of your servants is found with it shall die, and we also will be my lord's servants

dialogue.speaker: josephs-steward
dialogue.englishText: Let it be as you say: he with whom it is found shall be my servant, and the rest of you shall be innocent

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: What deed is this that you have done? Do you not know that a man like me can indeed practice divination?

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we clear ourselves? God has found out the guilt of your servants; behold, we are my lord's servants, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Far be it from me that I should do so! Only the man in whose hand the cup was found shall be my servant. But as for you, go up in peace to your father

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Oh, my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not your anger burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh himself

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: My lord asked his servants, saying, 'Have you a father or a brother?'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: And we said to my lord, 'We have a father, an old man, and a young child of his old age. His brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Then you said to your servants, 'Bring him down to me, that I may set my eyes on him'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: We said to my lord, 'The boy cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Then you said to your servants, 'Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall not see my face again'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: When we went back to your servant my father, we told him the words of my lord

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: And our father said, 'Go again, buy us a little food'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: We said, 'We cannot go down. If our youngest brother goes with us, then we will go down. For we cannot see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Then your servant my father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: One left me, and I said, Surely he has been torn to pieces, and I have never seen him since

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: If you take this one also from me, and harm happens to him, you will bring down my gray hairs in evil to Sheol

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, as soon as I come to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us, since his life is bound up in the boy's life

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: As soon as he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: For your servant became a pledge of safety for the boy to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father all my life'

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers

dialogue.speaker: judah
dialogue.englishText: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the evil that would find my father

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am Joseph! Is my father still alive?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Come near to me, please

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am Joseph your brother, whom you sold into Egypt

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And now do not be distressed or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: For the famine has been in the land these two years, and there are yet five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvest

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And God sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: So it was not you who sent me here, but God. He has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house and ruler over all the land of Egypt

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Hurry and go up to my father and say to him, 'Thus says your son Joseph: God has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me; do not tarry'

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: You shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children's children, and your flocks, your herds, and all that you have

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: There I will provide for you, for there are yet five years of famine to come, so that you and your household, and all that you have, do not come to poverty

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And behold, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin see, that it is my mouth that speaks to you

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: You must tell my father of all my honor in Egypt, and of all that you have seen. Hurry and bring my father down here

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Say to your brothers, 'Do this: load your animals and go back to the land of Canaan'

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: And take your father and your households, and come to me, and I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you shall eat the fat of the land

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: You are commanded to do this: take wagons from the land of Egypt for your little ones and for your wives, and bring your father, and come

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Have no concern for your goods, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Do not quarrel on the way

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: It is enough; Joseph my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: Jacob! Jacob!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Here I am.

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I am God, the God of your father. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for I will make you a great nation there.

dialogue.speaker: god
dialogue.englishText: I myself will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also bring you up again; and Joseph's hand will close your eyes.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Now let me die, since I have seen your face, because you are still alive.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I will go up and tell Pharaoh, and say to him: my brothers and my father's household who were in the land of Canaan have come to me.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: The men are shepherds, for they have been keepers of livestock; and they have brought their flocks and herds and all that they have.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: When Pharaoh calls you and asks: what is your occupation?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: You shall say: your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers — so that you may live in the land of Goshen, for every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they own, have come from the land of Canaan; and behold, they are in the land of Goshen.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: What is your occupation?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Your servants are shepherds, both we and our fathers.

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We have come to sojourn in the land, for there is no pasture for your servants' flocks, because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now please let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Your father and your brothers have come to you.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and brothers in the best of the land, let them dwell in the land of Goshen. And if you know able men among them, put them in charge of my livestock.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: How many are the days of the years of your life?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The days of the years of my sojourning are one hundred and thirty years; few and difficult have been the days of my life, and they have not attained to the days of my fathers in their sojourning.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: Give us food! Why should we die in your presence? For our money is gone.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Give your livestock, and I will give you food in exchange for your livestock, if the money is gone.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: We will not hide it from my lord: our money is all spent, and our livestock belong to my lord. Nothing is left before my lord but our bodies and our land.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: Why should we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land for bread, and we and our land will be servants to Pharaoh. Give us seed so that we may live and not die, and the land not become desolate.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have today bought you and your land for Pharaoh. Here is seed for you, so sow the land.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: At the harvests you shall give a fifth to Pharaoh, and four-fifths shall be your own, for seed for the field and for your food, for your households and as food for your little ones.

dialogue.speaker: egyptians
dialogue.englishText: You have saved our lives! Let us find favor in the sight of my lord, and we will be servants to Pharaoh.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: If now I have found favor in your sight, please put your hand under my thigh and deal with me in kindness and faithfulness. Please do not bury me in Egypt.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: But when I lie down with my fathers, you shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I will do as you have said.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Swear to me.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: He said to me: Behold, I will make you fruitful and multiply you, and I will make you a company of peoples, and I will give this land to your offspring after you as an everlasting possession.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: And now your two sons, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are mine; Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, as Reuben and Simeon are.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: And the children born to you after them shall be yours; they shall be called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: As for me, when I came from Paddan, Rachel died to my sorrow in the land of Canaan on the way, while there was still some distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Who are these?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: They are my sons, whom God has given me here.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Bring them to me, please, that I may bless them.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I did not expect to see your face, and behold, God has let me see your offspring also.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has been my shepherd all my life long to this day —

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: the angel who has redeemed me from all evil, bless the boys; and in them let my name be carried on, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Not so, my father, for this one is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I know, my son, I know. He also shall become a people, and he also shall be great. Nevertheless, his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: By you Israel will pronounce blessings, saying: God make you as Ephraim and as Manasseh.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I am about to die, but God will be with you and will bring you back to the land of your fathers.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Moreover, I have given to you rather than to your brothers one mountain slope that I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you what shall happen to you in days to come.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Assemble and listen, O sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might, and the firstfruits of my strength, preeminent in dignity and preeminent in power.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Unstable as water, you shall not excel, because you went up to your father's bed; then you defiled it — he went up to my couch!

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Simeon and Levi are brothers; weapons of violence are their swords.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Let my soul come not into their council; O my glory, be not joined to their company. For in their anger they killed men, and in their willfulness they hamstrung oxen.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce, and their wrath, for it is cruel! I will divide them in Jacob and scatter them in Israel.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Judah, your brothers shall praise you; your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; your father's sons shall bow down before you.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Judah is a lion's cub; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He stooped down; he crouched as a lion and as a lioness; who dares rouse him?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The scepter shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until Shiloh comes; and to him shall be the obedience of the peoples.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Binding his foal to the vine and his donkey's colt to the choice vine, he has washed his garments in wine and his vesture in the blood of grapes.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Zebulun shall dwell at the shore of the sea; he shall become a haven for ships, and his border shall be at Sidon.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Issachar is a strong donkey, crouching between the sheepfolds.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: He saw that a resting place was good, and that the land was pleasant, so he bowed his shoulder to bear and became a servant at forced labor.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Dan shall be a serpent in the way, a viper by the path, that bites the horse's heels so that his rider falls backward.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I wait for your salvation, O LORD.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Gad — a raiding band shall raid him, but he shall raid at their heels.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Asher's food shall be rich, and he shall yield royal delicacies.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Naphtali is a doe let loose that bears beautiful fawns.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Joseph is a fruitful bough, a fruitful bough by a spring; his branches run over the wall.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The archers bitterly attacked him, shot at him, and harassed him severely.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Yet his bow remained unmoved; his arms were made agile by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob — from there is the Shepherd, the Stone of Israel.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: By the God of your father who will help you, by the Almighty who will bless you — with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that crouches beneath, blessings of the breasts and of the womb.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The blessings of your father are mighty beyond the blessings of my parents, up to the bounties of the everlasting hills. May they be on the head of Joseph, and on the brow of him who was set apart from his brothers.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf, in the morning devouring the prey and at evening dividing the spoil.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: I am to be gathered to my people; bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: In the cave that is in the field at Machpelah, to the east of Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite to possess as a burying place.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: There they buried Abraham and Sarah his wife; there they buried Isaac and Rebekah his wife; and there I buried Leah.

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: The field and the cave that is in it were purchased from the Hittites.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in the hearing of Pharaoh, saying —

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: My father made me swear, saying: Behold, I am about to die; in my tomb which I dug for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me. Now therefore, let me please go up and bury my father; then I will return.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear.

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: What if Joseph bears a grudge against us and pays us back in full for all the wrong we did to him?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Your father gave a command before his death, saying —

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Thus you shall say to Joseph: Please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin, because they did evil to you. And now, please forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father.

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Behold, we are your servants.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to bring it about that many people should be kept alive, as they are today.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: So do not be afraid; I will provide for you and your little ones.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am about to die, but God will surely visit you and bring you up out of this land to the land that he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.
